insight: With Xaetron and Energy Matrix in the set, clicking Xaetron whenever possible yields ~2.85 BV per turn, while alternating absorb every 2 turns yields ~3 BV per turn, making alternation marginally better but dependent on inflation costs.
author: leary93
context: Discussion of optimal absorb strategy with Xaetron in sets with high-health absorbers.

insight: Xaetron's strength lies in generating free regeneration; the key is to enable free recovery when possible, which multiplies the effective absorb value compared to alternating strategies.
author: leary93
context: Strategic emphasis on Xaetron's core mechanic and how to maximize its value.

insight: Antima Comet combos effectively with Wincer, and a natural follow-up line is Wincer > Scorch > Drone into Antima if the set allows.
author: leary93
context: Discussion of unit synergy in opening sequences.

insight: Grimbotch with 1 lifespan should almost never be used offensively when taking damage; defending (2 damage soak value) is vastly superior to attacking (1 damage output), unless breaching or exploiting specific positions.
author: namington
context: Correction of fundamental unit usage mistake in beginner replay analysis.

insight: In aggressive sets with low efficient defense (called 'tactical' sets), Rhino absorb is devalued, but absorbing on Rhino still saves ~1 health per turn, translating to 2+ gold value when accounting for inflation.
author: namington
context: Explanation of why Rhino absorb matters even in low-defense scenarios.

insight: T5 Thunderhead into Oxide + Animus + DD enables efficient Cynestra spam; delaying or skipping T5 Thunderhead to avoid losing 2 Engineers is usually a losing strategy because the opponent must hold defensive units to stop it.
author: leary93
context: Analysis of tempo pressure and when to commit to aggressive builds despite short-term resource losses.

insight: Immolite increases Valkyrie's viability significantly; the line DD/DDC/DDB/DA+Oxide/Valk+Immolite sets up threatening builds and is cleaner than delayed Thunderhead pivots.
author: leary93
context: Discussion of synergistic unit combinations for specific set types.

insight: When Tarsier is the best attacker in the set, an Animus buying 10 Tarsiers is a strong economic play.
author: mrguy888
context: Justification of unit purchasing decisions in specific set contexts.

insight: Early Conduit for Forcefield generation is inefficient; a 4-cost non-inflationary Conduit to produce forcefields later yields worse value (6 gold + 2 drones) than a standard 8-gold defense solution.
author: namington
context: Cost-benefit analysis of unit purchasing in opening phases.

insight: Lancetooth counters Grimbotch efficiently and forces lower-econ games, but is hard to rush in low-econ sets; their existence paradoxically makes sets lower-econ, creating tension between their strength and viability.
author: namington
context: Meta-analysis of how specific unit presence shapes set economy and strategy.

insight: Inflation is the core mechanic governing unit purchasing decisions; it measures the compounding return on investment (ROI) of units over time. Drone inflation is approximately 4/3 (1.3333) per turn assuming free energy.
author: namington
context: Foundational explanation of inflation mechanics and drone ROI for beginner players.

insight: Tarsier rush is not automatically optimal despite superior inflation because early attackers are mostly nullified by absorbers; their primary function is breaking through the absorb barrier.
author: namington
context: Explanation of why attacker efficiency does not translate to automatic viability.

insight: Fission Turret has approximately 115% inflation relative to Drone and 105% relative to Tarsier without click value; including its click makes it highly efficient.
author: namington
context: Comparative inflation analysis of lifespan attackers.

insight: Bloodrager has exceptionally high inflation even when denying only 2 absorb; it may warrant a nerf but does not lead to degenerate games in practice.
author: namington
context: Balance assessment of absorb-denial units and their impact.

insight: Thorium Dynamo is very good but does not create degenerate gameplay despite high inflation; similar to Bloodrager in being overpowered without causing bad games.
author: namington
context: Balance discussion on units that are strong without warping game quality.

insight: Arka is exceptionally efficient (7 gold cost for powerful absorb), but a second Arka denies the first's prompt absorb if attack is under ~14 damage; the second player investing in Arka can neutralize the first player's advantage.
author: namington
context: Analysis of symmetrical unit interactions and counter-dynamics.

insight: Boreholes are more efficient than Tarsier in certain sets, making them preferable attackers when efficiency permits.
author: namington
context: Set-dependent attacker efficiency comparison.

insight: Vivid Drone was often superior to standard Drone in T2 when breach risk is low, providing better value without significant defensive downside.
author: namington
context: Discussion of unit substitutions in early-game economy building.

insight: Deadeye Operative should be bought in full (all 4 units) when attack is weak in the set; when attack is good, buying 1-2 Deadeye is viable only if running double blue tech without committing to double Steelsplitter.
author: crash_overlord
context: Strategic guidance on when to maximize Deadeye purchases.

insight: Savior is viable in BSO (Base Set Only) with a specific opening: DD/DD > DDE/DA > DDD/TTD > DDDB/TTD > WD1E/TTD > DDDE/TTB > WBEE/STT > WWEE/RRD > WWDDDDEEE (click Savior); P2 can counter with DD/DDE/DD1 fakeout.
author: f300xen
context: Base set economy breakthrough sequence using Savior.

insight: Centrifuge T2 is only worth pursuing if inflation is low and high-impact units (Vai, Odin) cannot be rushed otherwise; Lucent Hellion does not benefit as much from early Centrifuge since she generates her own green spending.
author: xujhan
context: Strategic analysis of when early Centrifuge justifies its cost.

insight: Endotherm Kit is only viable when inflation is low, the player wants to be in Chill colors anyway, and either chill is unusually efficient (lifespan defenders, breach vulnerability) or absorb is unusually bad.
author: xujhan
context: Conditions for viability of Chill-enabling unit.

insight: Elyot Animus (DD > A > subsequent play) loses to Cauterizer in most continuations; it is extremely tactics-heavy and unsuitable for beginners as bots exploit its vulnerabilities.
author: namington
context: Analysis of advanced P1 opening and why it is impractical for learning.

insight: Thorium Dynamo provides such efficient value that 3+ units are usually correct; building fewer than 3 Dynamos is rare unless the set has an overwhelming number of red rush units.
author: xujhan
context: General guidance on Thorium Dynamo quantity in standard game contexts.

insight: In sets with Resophage, Thorium Dynamo remains necessary (at least 1) for consistent green generation; a second Thorium is typically correct to maintain efficient wall generation, but additional Dynamos may not provide sufficient value.
author: shadourow
context: Discussion of Thorium Dynamo quantity in green-restricted sets.

insight: Sets are classified as 'tactical' when they feature aggressive attackers and low efficient defense, putting emphasis on exact health exchanges and narrow decision windows.
author: namington
context: Definition of meta-language used in set analysis and strategy discussion.
